title: Phone hardware impairment and 7800 position restructuring
decisionQuestion: Should Microsoft proceed with the asset impairment and position reduction in the Phone Hardware segment?
situation: Microsoft is addressing issues within its Phone Hardware segment, requiring an impairment charge and workforce reduction.
objectives: Reduce workforce by up to 7,800 positions.
Record a charge for asset and goodwill impairment.
conditions: An impairment adjustment of approximately $7.6 billion is required for the Phone Hardware segment.
The impairment relates specifically to the NDS business within the Phone Hardware segment.
constraints: The charge must be recorded in the fourth quarter of fiscal 2015.
The reduction must primarily affect the phone business.

options.description: Execute the full impairment adjustment and proceed with the 7,800 position reduction.
options.expectedBenefit: Achieve cost efficiency through asset write-down and workforce reduction.
options.principalRisk: Potential negative market reaction to the large impairment charge.

options.description: Implement only the position reduction without recording the full asset impairment charge.
options.expectedBenefit: Avoid the immediate recording of the $7.6 billion impairment charge.
options.principalRisk: Failure to fully reflect the segment's true financial health.

chosenAction: Execute the full impairment adjustment and proceed with the 7,800 position reduction.
rejectedBenefit: Avoiding the immediate recording of the $7.6 billion impairment charge.
observations: Microsoft announced the reduction of up to 7,800 positions in the phone business.
A charge for asset and goodwill impairment in the Phone Hardware segment is required.
The required impairment adjustment is approximately $7.6 billion.
inferences: The impairment adjustment is necessary because the Phone Hardware segment's assets and goodwill are overstated.
The decision to reduce positions is a direct response to the financial strain indicated by the impairment.

evidenceSpans.exactQuote: Microsoft also announced the reduction of up to 7,800 positions, primarily in the phone business.
Microsoft will record a charge in the fourth quarter of fiscal 2015 for the impairment of assets and goodwill in its Phone Hardware segment, related to the NDS business.
Accordingly, the company concluded that an impairment adjustment of its Phone Hardware segment assets and goodwill of approximately $7.6 billion is required.
